title: TeddyBank | Financial Literacy Platform
description: Winner of the Wealthsimple Challenge at the ElleHacks Hackathon (York University). TeddyBank is a full-stack IoT financial-literacy platform that connects physical hardware with a cloud dashboard to make money tangible and interactive for kids. It transforms transaction data into gamified tasks, stock simulations, and AI-generated multilingual stories delivered through voice. The project explores how conversational interfaces and tactile interaction can teach earning, saving, and investing in an engaging way.

title: SquatCAM | Clinical Exercise Monitoring
description: Winner of 'Most Ethical Solution' at the UWaterloo HealthTech Innovation Challenge. SquatCAM is a vision-based rehabilitation monitoring platform developed for CCCARE (Centre for Community, Clinical and Applied Research Excellence) that uses computer vision to recognize exercises, count repetitions, and stream physiological data to a real-time clinician dashboard. Designed with privacy-first principles, it leverages biometric embeddings for identification and edge processing to keep sensitive video data local while enabling kinesiologists to supervise multiple patients and receive safety alerts.

title: Jeep Model and Assembly
description: Modeled a multi-component assembly in SolidWorks, including a chassis, independent door panels, and wheel systems, utilizing standard mates to define mechanical relationships between moving parts. Generated technical engineering drawings featuring exploded views and cross-sections to visualize internal component clearances and communicate the step-by-step assembly process.

title: Pubmed RAG Researcher
description: An AI-powered research assistant designed to accelerate systematic reviews. It automates literature retrieval from Pubmed, generating concise summaries of key findings and identifying the best article for immediate reading. The tool features an interactive RAG chatbot to talk with the data and exports all insights as a structured PDF report.

title: Dynamic Motion Translation Mechanism
description: Engineered a kinetic mechanical flower assembly in SolidWorks, utilizing crossed helical gears to translate rotational input into the synchronized blooming of multiple petals. Conducted rigorous tolerance analysis to optimize component fit for manufacturing, ensuring smooth mechanical operation with minimal friction. Fabricated and assembled physical prototypes, iterating on gear geometry to maximize power transmission efficiency and structural integrity.

title: Accessible Product Redesign (Settlers of Catan)
description: Engineered a modular, magnetically coupled game board to accommodate users with neurological tremors. Performed detailed CAD modeling in SolidWorks to design a custom system that prevents piece displacement. Utilized Quality Function Deployment (QFD) to translate user needs into CAD constraints and validated the physical design using a digital force gauge (100 Hz sampling) and regression analysis.

title: TimeSense | Productivity and Analytics App
description: A native mobile app built in Java to improve focus and tracking. It utilizes a foreground service to monitor usage patterns and actively block distracting applications. The app uses local storage and SQLite to log session data, allowing users to compare their planned schedule against actual performance.

title: BrickbyBrick | Full-Stack FinTech Platform
description: Winner of 'Best Use of Snowflake API' at the SheHacks Hackathon (Western University). Created a fractional real estate investment platform designed to lower the barrier to homeownership. Built with a Python backend and JavaScript frontend, it utilizes a database pipeline to bridge Snowflake housing data with MongoDB user records. The system allows investors to purchase property shares while enabling homeowners to list assets and keep a minimum of 51% ownership, tracking all transactions in a real-time portfolio dashboard.

title: StoryBridge | Multi-Model AI Orchestration Pipeline
description: Winner of 'Most Innovative Feature' at the Waterloo Women in Engineering Hackathon. Developed an end-to-end AI pipeline in Python that transforms seniors' spoken memories into illustrated comic narratives. Integrated Deepgram for speech-to-text (NLP), Moonshot AI for summarization, and FLUX.1 for computer vision image generation to support memory care in healthcare settings.

title: Wheel of Fortune Game
description: A desktop game application built entirely in Java. It features a custom graphical interface (GUI) and a persistent high-score system powered by local file I/O. The project demonstrates strong Object-Oriented design, utilizing multi-threading for animations and complex string manipulation algorithms for the puzzle logic.

title: Methane Adaptive Capture System for Tailings Ponds
description: Developed an eco-mimicry-inspired robotic solution to track and capture shifting methane hotspots in industrial tailings ponds. By leveraging autonomous navigation and real-time fleet management software, the system provides a high-efficiency alternative to traditional emissions monitoring. The project involved rigorous financial modeling of a 15-year lifecycle and the engineering of intrinsically safe hardware to meet global IECEx standards, successfully transforming a volatile greenhouse gas into a controlled, lower-impact emission.
